$comment: c81 (the sqlite shell.c CLI route): ship <signal.h> — signal() + raise() symbols and the SIG* numbers as PP-VISIBLE macros (shell.c #ifdef-guards every handler registration, so a semantic-only `constants` injection would silently compile the handlers out — the #ifdef SIGINT arm in main.c pins the PP visibility). signal's handler param/return are typed fn-pointers (ptr<fn(i32) -> void>, the stdlib.json qsort-comparator precedent); the bare-designator argument is shell.c's exact shape. Run-proof: raise(SIGINT) delivers synchronously through real libc, the handler sets the volatile flag, main returns 42. Values pinned: SIGHUP=1 SIGINT=2 SIGTRAP=5 SIGTERM=15 (glibc signum-generic.h = Darwin BSD numbering, flat). Gated [elf,macho] (shell.c's include is !_WIN32; the pe surface diverges — no SIGHUP/SIGTRAP). RED-ON-DISABLE: remove signal.json -> F001A; demote the macros to constants -> exit 1.
